questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red; stop if you can do so safely
Stop and wait for a green arrow
The signal is out of service
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is about to turn red, so you should stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
choices: Triangle
Octagon (eight sides)
Diamond
Round
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always an eight-sided red octagon, a shape used for no other sign.

questions.prompt: How should you treat a flashing red traffic signal?
choices: The same as a stop sign
The same as a green light
As a yield sign you may roll through
As a signal that is broken and can be ignored
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means come to a complete stop and proceed only when it is safe, just like a stop sign.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the road means:
choices: You may pass when the way is clear
Passing is not allowed on your side
The lane is for left turns only
You may park along the line
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side of the center marking means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: What color and shape are most warning signs that alert you to conditions ahead?
choices: Red octagon
Yellow diamond
Green rectangle
Blue square
questions.explanation: Most warning signs are yellow diamonds that alert you to hazards or changes in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does an orange sign or cone usually indicate?
choices: A school zone
A construction or work zone
A no-passing zone
A scenic route
questions.explanation: Orange signs and cones mark construction and maintenance work zones, where you should slow down and stay alert.

questions.prompt: A green arrow signal pointing left means:
choices: You may turn left and oncoming traffic is stopped for you
Yield to oncoming traffic before turning left
Left turns are prohibited
Stop before turning left
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected turn, meaning conflicting traffic is being held by a red light.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Traffic moving in opposite directions
Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
The road from the shoulder only
Bicycle lanes from sidewalks only
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark because of a power failure, you should:
choices: Proceed without stopping
Treat the intersection as an all-way stop
Wait for a police officer before moving
Honk and drive through
questions.explanation: When signals are dark, treat the intersection as a four-way stop and yield in turn.

questions.prompt: A pennant-shaped (sideways triangle) sign on the left side of the road means:
choices: No passing zone
Yield ahead
Divided highway ends
Dead end
questions.explanation: The pennant-shaped sign marks the start of a no-passing zone.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, two vehicles arrive at the same time. Who has the right of way?
choices: The vehicle on the left
The vehicle on the right
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an intersection at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are making a left turn at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Only vehicles behind you
Oncoming traffic and pedestrians crossing your path
No one if you have a green light
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A driver turning left must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: When you approach a roundabout, you must:
choices: Stop and wait for all traffic to clear
Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Speed up to merge
Always come to a complete stop before entering
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering a roundabout must yield to traffic already circulating within it.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian is in a marked crosswalk ahead of you. You should:
choices: Honk so they hurry
Stop and yield the right of way
Drive around them
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians within a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue driving normally
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: In Iowa, a left turn on a red light is allowed only when:
choices: Turning from a one-way street onto another one-way street
Turning onto any street with light traffic
Turning at night
It is never allowed
questions.explanation: A left turn on red is permitted only from a one-way street onto another one-way street, after stopping and yielding.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal your intention?
choices: Right as you begin the turn
At least 100 feet before the turn
Only if other cars are present
After you start braking
questions.explanation: You should signal at least 100 feet before turning to give other drivers time to react.

questions.prompt: When entering a road from a private driveway or alley, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic and pedestrians on the road
Proceed because driveways have priority
Honk and merge quickly
Stop only if a sign is posted
questions.explanation: A driver entering a roadway from a driveway or alley must yield to all approaching traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: When you reach an uncontrolled intersection at the same time as a car already crossing it, you should:
choices: Force your way through
Yield to the vehicle already in the intersection
Honk to claim the right of way
Accelerate to beat them
questions.explanation: You must yield to any vehicle that has already entered the intersection ahead of you.

questions.prompt: At a 'T' intersection without signals, who generally has the right of way?
choices: Traffic on the road that ends (the stem)
Traffic on the through road (the top of the T)
Whoever honks first
The vehicle turning left
questions.explanation: Drivers on the road that ends must yield to traffic on the continuing through road.

questions.prompt: Unless posted otherwise, the statutory speed limit in an Iowa residential or school district is:
choices: 15 mph
25 mph
35 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Iowa's default speed limit in residential and school districts is 25 mph unless a sign states otherwise.

questions.prompt: The speed limit on most Iowa rural interstate highways is:
choices: 55 mph
65 mph
70 mph
80 mph
questions.explanation: Most rural interstate highways in Iowa have a speed limit of 70 mph.

questions.prompt: What is a recommended minimum following distance behind the vehicle ahead under good conditions?
choices: One second
Three to four seconds
Half a second
Ten car lengths regardless of speed
questions.explanation: A following distance of at least three to four seconds gives you time to react and stop safely.

questions.prompt: Unless posted otherwise, the default speed limit on most open Iowa rural highways is:
choices: 45 mph
55 mph
65 mph
70 mph
questions.explanation: The statutory speed limit on most rural Iowa highways is 55 mph unless signs indicate otherwise.

questions.prompt: When driving in rain, snow, or fog, you should:
choices: Maintain the posted limit no matter what
Reduce speed and increase your following distance
Follow closely to see better
Use your high beams in fog
questions.explanation: Poor conditions require slower speeds and more space to compensate for reduced traction and visibility.

questions.prompt: Even when you are driving at the posted speed limit, Iowa law requires you to:
choices: Drive at a speed that is reasonable for current conditions
Always drive exactly at the limit
Match the speed of the fastest car
Drive 5 mph under the limit at all times
questions.explanation: You must always drive at a speed reasonable for traffic, weather, and road conditions, even if it is below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: How does higher speed affect your total stopping distance?
choices: It has no effect
It greatly increases stopping distance
It shortens stopping distance
It only affects braking on hills
questions.explanation: As speed increases, both reaction distance and braking distance grow, sharply increasing total stopping distance.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance beyond the normal three to four seconds?
choices: When the road is dry and clear
When being tailgated or in poor conditions
When traffic is light
Never; three seconds is always enough
questions.explanation: Add more space when conditions are poor or a driver is following you too closely so you can brake gradually.

questions.prompt: Unless posted otherwise, the statutory speed limit in an Iowa business district is:
choices: 20 mph
30 mph
40 mph
55 mph
questions.explanation: Iowa's default speed limit in a business district is 20 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, which lane should you normally drive in?
choices: The left lane
The right lane, using the left only to pass
Whichever lane is emptiest
The center turn lane
questions.explanation: Keep right except when passing so faster traffic can use the left lane to overtake.

questions.prompt: You may NOT pass another vehicle when:
choices: The road is straight and clear
You are within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing
There is a broken yellow line on your side
Traffic is light
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited within 100 feet of intersections and railroad crossings because of limited visibility and crossing traffic.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Rely only on your mirrors
Signal, check mirrors, and glance over your shoulder to check blind spots
Change lanes quickly without signaling
Honk and merge
questions.explanation: Always signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder to clear blind spots before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When another driver is passing you, you should:
choices: Speed up to prevent the pass
Maintain or slightly reduce your speed
Move into the left lane
Brake hard
questions.explanation: Hold your speed steady or slow slightly so the passing driver can complete the maneuver and return safely.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when is it safe to return to your lane?
choices: As soon as you are alongside the vehicle
When you can see both of the passed vehicle's headlights in your mirror
After 30 seconds
Only at the next exit
questions.explanation: Returning when you can see both headlights in your mirror ensures you have left enough room ahead of the passed vehicle.

questions.prompt: A double solid yellow line in the center of the road means:
choices: Passing is allowed in both directions
Passing is not allowed in either direction
The left lane is a turn lane
You may pass only at night
questions.explanation: Double solid yellow lines mean neither direction of traffic may cross to pass.

questions.prompt: A center lane marked with two-way left-turn arrows and yellow lines is used for:
choices: Passing slow vehicles
Left turns from either direction
Parking during business hours
Emergency vehicles only
questions.explanation: A center two-way left-turn lane is shared by both directions for making left turns, not for through travel or passing.

questions.prompt: You should avoid passing on which of the following?
choices: A flat, straight road with a broken yellow line
A hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
A passing lane with a posted sign
A multilane road in the left lane
questions.explanation: Never pass on hills or curves because you cannot see oncoming traffic far enough ahead to pass safely.

questions.prompt: When may you pass a stopped school bus that has its red lights flashing and stop arm out?
choices: At no time until the lights stop flashing
If you slow to 20 mph
If no children are visible
If you are in a hurry
questions.explanation: You must stop and may not pass a school bus loading or unloading with red lights flashing until the lights are turned off.

questions.prompt: Iowa's Move Over or Slow Down law requires you to do what when approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights?
choices: Maintain your speed
Move over a lane if possible, or slow down if you cannot
Stop completely in your lane
Speed up to pass quickly
questions.explanation: The Move Over or Slow Down law requires changing lanes away from stopped vehicles with flashing lights, or slowing down when you cannot move over.

questions.prompt: Before turning or changing lanes, why should you check twice for motorcycles?
choices: They are required to yield
They are small and can be hidden in blind spots
They always travel faster than cars
They have no headlights
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and easily hidden in blind spots, so checking twice helps you avoid a collision.

questions.prompt: When following a large truck, you should remember that:
choices: Trucks can stop faster than cars
Trucks have large blind spots and need more room to stop
Trucks always yield to cars
It is safe to drive close behind them
questions.explanation: Large trucks have big blind spots and longer stopping distances, so give them extra space.

questions.prompt: An orange triangle on the back of a vehicle indicates:
choices: A police vehicle
A slow-moving vehicle traveling under 25 mph
A hazardous load
A funeral procession
questions.explanation: The orange triangle marks a slow-moving vehicle, such as farm equipment, traveling under 25 mph.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Pass closely to save time
Give them room and pass only when safe
Honk to make them move off the road
Expect them to ride on the sidewalk
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have a right to the road, so give them space and pass only when you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: When a truck makes a right turn, it may:
choices: Stay tight against the curb
Swing wide to the left before turning right
Turn faster than a car
Never use turn signals
questions.explanation: Large trucks often swing wide to complete a turn, so never try to squeeze between the truck and the curb.

questions.prompt: If you cannot see a truck driver in the truck's side mirrors, it means:
choices: The driver can still see you
The driver probably cannot see you
The truck is parked
You are at a safe distance
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the driver's mirrors, you are in a blind spot and the driver likely cannot see you.

questions.prompt: When you encounter farm equipment on a rural Iowa road, you should:
choices: Pass immediately regardless of conditions
Slow down and pass only when it is safe and legal
Tailgate to encourage it to speed up
Drive onto the shoulder to pass
questions.explanation: Farm equipment moves slowly, so reduce your speed and pass only where it is legal and you have clear sight distance.

questions.prompt: In Iowa, the blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit for a driver age 21 or older is:
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are legally intoxicated at a BAC of 0.08 or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Iowa's zero-tolerance law, a driver under age 21 is in violation at a BAC of:
choices: 0.08 or higher
0.02 or higher
0.05 or higher
0.10 or higher
questions.explanation: Iowa's zero-tolerance law makes it illegal for drivers under 21 to drive with a BAC of 0.02 or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Iowa's hands-free law, while driving you may:
choices: Hold your phone to type a text
Use the phone only in a hands-free or voice-activated mode
Scroll social media at red lights
Make handheld video calls
questions.explanation: Iowa's hands-free law bans holding a device while driving; you may only use it in hands-free or voice-activated mode.

questions.prompt: What is the only effective way to lower your blood alcohol concentration?
choices: Drinking coffee
Taking a cold shower
Time
Eating a large meal
questions.explanation: Only time allows your body to remove alcohol; coffee, food, and showers do not speed up the process.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving includes any activity that:
choices: Keeps your eyes on the road
Takes your eyes, hands, or mind off driving
Improves your reaction time
Only involves loud music
questions.explanation: A distraction is anything that takes your eyes off the road, hands off the wheel, or mind off the task of driving.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your driving ability?
choices: It improves concentration
It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It has no effect in small amounts
It sharpens vision
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reaction time and impairs judgment, coordination, and vision, making driving dangerous.

questions.prompt: The commercial driver BAC limit in Iowa is:
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers are held to a stricter BAC limit of 0.04.

questions.prompt: To reduce distraction, you should set up your navigation and music:
choices: While driving on the highway
Before you begin driving
At every stop sign
Only after you get lost
questions.explanation: Setting up navigation and audio before you start driving keeps your attention on the road.

questions.prompt: Besides alcohol, which of the following can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Prescription and over-the-counter medications that cause drowsiness
Drinking water
Wearing sunglasses
Listening to the radio quietly
questions.explanation: Many legal medications can cause drowsiness or impairment, so always check labels before driving.

questions.prompt: Iowa law requires you to use your headlights:
choices: Only on the interstate
From sunset to sunrise and when visibility is reduced
Only when other cars are present
Never during the day
questions.explanation: Headlights are required from sunset to sunrise and whenever weather or conditions reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: A good rule of thumb in Iowa is to turn on your headlights whenever:
choices: You use your windshield wipers
You turn on the radio
You exceed 45 mph
The car ahead does
questions.explanation: Using headlights whenever your wipers are on keeps you visible during rain, snow, and fog.

questions.prompt: In foggy conditions, you should use:
choices: High-beam headlights
Low-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving
questions.explanation: Low beams reduce glare in fog, while high beams reflect off the fog and reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road is most likely to freeze first in cold weather?
choices: Bridges and overpasses
Hilltops
Gravel shoulders
Parking lots
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from both sides and freeze before other road surfaces.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Slam on the brakes
Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Steer sharply in the opposite direction
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: To recover from a skid, ease off the accelerator and gently steer toward where you want the front of the car to go.

questions.prompt: Why is the road often most slippery during the first few minutes of rain?
choices: The rain washes the road clean
Water mixes with oil and dust to form a slick film
Tires grip better when wet
The pavement cools too quickly
questions.explanation: Early rain mixes with oil and dirt on the pavement to create a slippery film before it washes away.

questions.prompt: When driving at night, you should:
choices: Use high beams at all times
Dim your high beams for oncoming traffic and when following closely
Drive faster to get home sooner
Look directly at oncoming headlights
questions.explanation: Dim your high beams for oncoming vehicles and when closely following others to avoid blinding them.

questions.prompt: If you encounter deep standing water or flooding across the road, you should:
choices: Drive through quickly
Turn around and find another route
Follow the car ahead closely
Speed up to create a wake
questions.explanation: Never drive into water of unknown depth; it can stall your vehicle or sweep it away, so turn around.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
choices: 5 feet
10 feet
15 feet
25 feet
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so firefighters can reach it.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning your wheels toward the curb when facing downhill keeps the car from rolling into traffic if the brakes fail.

questions.prompt: Under Iowa law, a child younger than one year old and under 20 pounds must ride in:
choices: A forward-facing car seat
A rear-facing child restraint
A booster seat
A regular seat belt
questions.explanation: Iowa law requires a child under one year and under 20 pounds to ride in a rear-facing child restraint.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
Either direction is fine
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the tire rests against it if the car rolls back.

questions.prompt: You may park in a marked accessible (disabled) parking space only if:
choices: You will be quick
You display a valid disability permit or plate
It is the only space available
You turn on your hazard lights
questions.explanation: Accessible spaces are reserved for vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or license plate.

questions.prompt: In Iowa, seat belt and child restraint use is required for:
choices: Only the driver
Only front-seat passengers
All occupants, with children properly secured by restraint or belt
Only children under age 6
questions.explanation: Iowa requires occupants to be secured, with children up to age 18 in an appropriate restraint or seat belt.

questions.prompt: You should not park within how many feet of a stop sign?
choices: 10 feet
20 feet
30 feet
50 feet
questions.explanation: Parking within 30 feet of a stop sign or signal is prohibited because it can block the view of the sign.

questions.prompt: After parking your vehicle on any hill, you should always:
choices: Leave it in neutral
Set the parking brake and shift to park
Leave the engine running
Leave the wheels straight
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and shifting to park helps keep the vehicle from rolling on a hill.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for an instruction permit in Iowa?
choices: 14 years old
15 years old
16 years old
18 years old
questions.explanation: In Iowa you may apply for an instruction permit at age 14 after passing the vision and knowledge tests.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving must an Iowa instruction permit holder complete?
choices: 10 hours
20 hours, including 2 at night
40 hours
50 hours
questions.explanation: Iowa requires at least 20 hours of supervised driving, with a minimum of two hours at night, on an instruction permit.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Iowa knowledge (written) test, and what score is needed to pass?
choices: 25 questions, 70% to pass
35 questions, 80% to pass
40 questions, 75% to pass
50 questions, 90% to pass
questions.explanation: The Iowa knowledge test has 35 questions, and you must answer at least 80 percent correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: While driving with an Iowa instruction permit, you must:
choices: Always be supervised by a qualified adult
Drive alone after 30 days
Drive only on weekends
Carry no passengers ever
questions.explanation: An instruction permit holder must always be accompanied by a qualified, licensed adult while driving.

questions.prompt: An Iowa intermediate license restricts unsupervised driving during which hours?
choices: Between 9 p.m. and 6 a.m.
Between 12:30 a.m. and 5 a.m.
Between 11 p.m. and 7 a.m.
There is no time restriction
questions.explanation: Intermediate license holders may not drive unsupervised between 12:30 a.m. and 5 a.m. unless an exception applies.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for an Iowa intermediate license?
choices: 14 years old
15 years old
16 years old
17 years old
questions.explanation: You may apply for an intermediate license at age 16 after holding an instruction permit and meeting other requirements.

questions.prompt: Before moving from an instruction permit to an intermediate license, you generally must hold the permit for at least:
choices: 1 month
6 months
12 months
24 months
questions.explanation: Iowa requires holding the instruction permit for at least 12 months with a clean record before an intermediate license.

questions.prompt: What tests must you pass to get an Iowa instruction permit?
choices: Only a driving test
The vision and knowledge tests
Only a vision test
A physical fitness test
questions.explanation: To get an instruction permit you must pass both the vision test and the written knowledge test.

questions.prompt: Under Iowa's implied consent law, refusing a chemical test when lawfully requested results in:
choices: A warning only
An automatic license suspension
No penalty
A reduced fine
questions.explanation: By driving in Iowa you consent to chemical testing, and refusing a lawful test leads to an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, Iowa law requires you to:
choices: Leave if no police arrive
Stop and exchange information
Continue driving if the damage is minor
Move your car and drive away
questions.explanation: You must stop at the scene of a crash and exchange information; leaving can result in serious charges.

questions.prompt: In Iowa, driving is considered:
choices: A guaranteed right
A privilege that can be suspended or revoked
Automatic once you turn 18
Permanent once licensed
questions.explanation: Driving is a privilege, and unsafe or illegal behavior can lead to suspension or revocation of your license.

questions.prompt: Violating Iowa's Move Over or Slow Down law can result in:
choices: No penalty
A scheduled fine, with harsher penalties if it causes a crash
Only a warning
A reward
questions.explanation: Failing to move over or slow down carries a fine, and penalties increase sharply if the violation causes a crash.

questions.prompt: Iowa requires every driver to carry proof of:
choices: Vehicle color
Financial responsibility (insurance)
Fuel receipts
A spare tire
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry proof of financial responsibility, typically liability insurance, and show it when required.

questions.prompt: A driver who violates Iowa's hands-free phone law faces:
choices: No consequences
Fines that increase if the violation causes injury or death
Only a verbal warning forever
A free phone mount
questions.explanation: Hands-free violations carry fines, and those fines rise significantly when the violation results in injury or death.

questions.prompt: What can happen if a driver accumulates too many traffic violations or convictions?
choices: Nothing changes
Their driving privileges can be suspended or revoked
They get a longer license
They receive a discount on fines
questions.explanation: Accumulating violations or serious convictions can lead to suspension or revocation of your driving privileges.

questions.prompt: Leaving the scene of a crash involving injury in Iowa is:
choices: A minor infraction
A serious offense that can bring criminal charges
Allowed if you call later
Permitted with no penalty
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of an injury crash is a serious offense that can result in criminal charges and loss of your license.
